lastReleaseMessage: Restore dates.ts after a botched regex replacement in the...

barRaise.topFinding: Eleventh straight daily run, fifth consecutive byte-identical tree, same single HIGH. Verified from disk: HEAD is still 890b493 (2026-06-13, five days cold), git diff --stat unchanged at 4 files/13 insertions/6 deletions, the same seven git status --porcelain entries as 06-15 through 06-17, and main level with origin/main (0 0), so nothing external blocks a commit. The uncommitted cohort is the same doc fix (CHANGELOG date corrections, DESIGN.md Yes& rebrand) plus the FUSE git-safety subsystem (scripts/git-guard.ps1 and its wiring) sitting outside the repo where a bad checkout erases it. No new code signal because no code moved; at run eleven the report itself is the waste, re-deriving one ship recommendation that has ridden nine reports unactioned. One release.ps1 run closes the HIGH and reverts the verdict to healthy; pausing bar-raise-scheduler-daily until HEAD moves stops the re-derivation.

actions.title: Ship the whole working tree in one pass: `cd X:\YesAndScheduler; .\scripts\release.ps1` (or `git add -A; git commit`). Lands the doc cohort and `git-guard.ps1` inside the repo and refreshes the dashboard. Nothing external blocks it. Closes the reliability HIGH, the audit-loop MEDIUM, and most of the FUSE/maintainability LOWs at once.
Pause `bar-raise-scheduler-daily`, or drop it to twice-weekly, until HEAD moves. The recommendation has ridden seven reports; apply it to the task registry instead of re-deriving it an eleventh time.
